mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero indicates extreme monetary accommodation. This depresses discount rates and supports asset valuation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as long as no deflation trap emerges. The signal is always active but most powerful during accommodation.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 1% are not significantly positive at the 95% confidence level (one-sided t-test).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% signals severe labor market slack, which depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings. This triggers a negative outlook for equities over the subsequent month as the economy struggles to recover.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day forward return after UNRATE > 8% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test, p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both consumer prices are stable (CPI below 218) and the federal funds rate is essentially zero, the economy faces deflation risk and a liquidity trap. This unusual combination depresses expected inflation and nominal growth, leading to negative equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day forward return in triggered periods is greater than zero at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions following severe recessions, rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand and a self-sustaining recovery. This positively surprises risk premia and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon. The mechanism is only effective in the early recovery regime.
failure_condition: The rank IC of RSAFS growth on 20-day forward returns during expansion periods remains below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below a severe recessionary threshold (index < 95) indicates chronic underutilization of capacity and contracting output. This points to persistent weakness in corporate earnings, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the economy struggles to regain momentum.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances triggered by INDPRO < 95.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment falling and staying below 130 million signals extreme labor market distress. Such a low level of employment implies weak consumer income and demand, which is immediately reflected in negative short-term equity returns as markets price in continued economic headwinds.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after PAYEMS < 130000 are positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP is elevated above a high threshold and consumer prices are also elevated, the economy may be overheating. This combination pressures central banks to tighten policy, which in turn depresses equity valuations over the following quarter. The interaction of strong demand and rising inflation creates a negative forward return signal.
failure_condition: The mean 60-day forward return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (p < 0.10, one-sided).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts reflect early-stage demand and credit conditions. An elevated level of housing starts signals improving economic sentiment and investment, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the construction pipeline feeds into broader growth.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between the level of HOUST and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly positive at the 95% confidence level.
